evals.expected_output: Scan completes, presents totals per tier, uses AskUserQuestion for each non-empty category (AUTO-BLOAT / LIKELY-BLOAT / AMBIGUOUS), moves approved items to Desktop/.housekeeping-quarantine/{today}/ with manifest.json, runs purge_quarantine for entries >7d old, runs validate_output, reports MB recovered.

assertions.name: scan_runs
assertions.description: scan.py executes and writes scan-report.json without touching filesystem

assertions.name: no_protected_in_report
assertions.description: scan-report.json contains zero items with path matching PROTECTED_NAMES (SKILL.md, pillars.md, idea-bank.json, etc.)

assertions.name: user_prompted_per_tier
assertions.description: AskUserQuestion invoked once per non-empty tier; user decisions recorded in approved-plan.json

assertions.name: cleanup_moves_not_deletes
assertions.description: cleanup.py only moves to quarantine; original paths exist in .housekeeping-quarantine/{date}/items/

assertions.name: manifest_written
assertions.description: manifest.json written with one entry per moved item (original_path, quarantine_path, size_bytes, sha256, timestamp)

evals.prompt: rollback yesterday's cleanup of the mining folder
evals.expected_output: rollback.py --date YYYY-MM-DD --pattern 'voice-samples*' restores the specific files to original paths. Log entry written. Manifest pruned of restored entries. Destination conflicts reported but not overwritten.

assertions.name: rollback_restores_to_original
assertions.description: Files move from quarantine back to their original paths listed in manifest

assertions.name: conflict_fails_loudly
assertions.description: If destination already exists, rollback reports CONFLICT and does NOT overwrite

assertions.name: manifest_updated
assertions.description: After restore, manifest.json no longer lists the restored entries

evals.expected_output: install_launchagent.py writes ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.mayank.housekeeping.plist with StartCalendarInterval Saturday 10:00, loads via launchctl, verifies with launchctl list.

assertions.name: plist_written
assertions.description: plist file exists with Weekday=6, Hour=10, Minute=0

assertions.name: launchctl_load_succeeds
assertions.description: launchctl load returns 0 and label appears in launchctl list

assertions.name: fires_weekly_nudge
assertions.description: plist invokes weekly_nudge.py (not cleanup.py directly — cron must not silently delete)

evals.expected_output: If approved-plan.json accidentally contains a PROTECTED path (e.g., pillars.md), cleanup.py REFUSES the move and logs 'REFUSED (PROTECTED: ...)'. No touch to protected file.

assertions.name: cleanup_defense_in_depth
assertions.description: cleanup.py.is_protected() double-checks each approved path; protected ones logged REFUSED

assertions.name: protected_file_untouched
assertions.description: Protected path still exists at original location after cleanup run

evals.expected_output: purge_quarantine.py identifies date-folders older than 7 days, permanently deletes them via shutil.rmtree, logs size + path. Defense: only operates inside QUARANTINE_ROOT. Refuses paths outside.

assertions.name: only_old_folders_purged
assertions.description: Date-folders <=7d old are kept; >7d are purged

assertions.name: never_purges_non_date_folders
assertions.description: Non-YYYY-MM-DD folder names inside quarantine are skipped (e.g., stray config)

assertions.name: logs_permanent_deletion
assertions.description: Every purge logged with byte total to housekeeping.log

assertions.name: refuses_path_outside_quarantine_root
assertions.description: If somehow path escapes QUARANTINE_ROOT, purge refuses with REFUSED log
